$comment: The end-of-turn review hop's constrained output (the checkpoint layer's ONLY model pass), extended for 015. Flat + closed (Constitution II). The hop CLASSIFIES; verdict mapping, provenance, cooldown identity, and all flag wording are server-assembled pure functions. violated_preference is a verbatim echo used only to map back to the mined candidate (best lexical overlap) - the flag's quoted content, memory id, and trust standing come from the server-held candidate, never from this echo. Decline bias on both judgments: uncertain => false.
name: checkpoint_review

contradicts.description: A real, explicit, material contradiction exists among the candidate pairs. Uncertain => false.

statement_a.description: The earlier statement, verbatim. Empty when contradicts is false.

statement_b.description: The final statement, verbatim. Empty when contradicts is false.

basis.description: One sentence: why both cannot hold (or the strongest reason the candidates are consistent).

violates.description: The turn (final message and/or its observed activity) violates one of the listed stored preferences. Uncertain => false; a preference too vague to check concretely => false.

violated_preference.description: Verbatim echo of the single most material violated preference from the numbered list. Empty when violates is false. Server-side map-back only.

violation_basis.description: One sentence: what in the turn violates it. Empty when violates is false.

fixed_flag_templates.self_contradiction: End-of-turn review: your conclusion contradicts an earlier committed statement. Earlier: "<statement_a>" - Final: "<statement_b>". Basis: <basis> Reconcile the two explicitly before finishing.
fixed_flag_templates.preference_violation: End-of-turn review: this turn appears to violate a stored preference: "<candidate.content>" (memory <candidate.memory_id>, <trust> provenance). Basis: <violation_basis> Revise the response to honor it, or state explicitly why it does not apply here.
fixed_flag_templates.both: <self_contradiction template> <preference_violation template> (concatenated, contradiction first; one flag, two signals, independent cooldown keys)
